description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in `WebAssemblyValidateImpl` (src/wasm/wasm-js.cc). The function first calls `GetFirstArgumentAsBytes` to extract a raw pointer into the ArrayBuffer's backing store, storing it in a `base::Vector<const uint8_t>`. It then calls `ArgumentToCompileOptions(info[1], ...)` to process the `options` argument, which may invoke `JSReceiver::GetProperty` on a Proxy object, thereby triggering arbitrary JavaScript execution via the Proxy's `get` trap. An attacker can use this callback window to call `buffer.transfer()` to detach the original ArrayBuffer (severing it from its backing store) and then `gc()` to free the backing store. When `WasmEngine::SyncValidate` is subsequently called with the now-stale `bytes` vector, it reads from freed memory. The root cause is a TOCTOU race: the raw data pointer is obtained before the `options` argument is processed, but processing `options` can trigger JS execution that invalidates the pointer through detachment and garbage collection of the ArrayBuffer's backing store.

fixes.description: Patch generated from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6865998 to avoid remote fetch during validation.
fixes.evidence_text: 4293cb47afcbb1575f51e1cbd63c7cf8f21f85ff [wasm] WA.validate(): always copy the wire bytes
